sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Both Espanyol and Las Palmas are in poor form, with Espanyol losing all of their last 5 matches and Las Palmas also losing their last 5. Espanyol have struggled to score and defend, conceding in all recent games, while Las Palmas have failed to score in their last 3 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results have been tight with low-scoring outcomes (1-0 to Las Palmas and 2-1 to Espanyol). Matches between these teams often feature under 2.5 goals, as seen in 5 of their last 7 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Espanyol (17th, 39 points) have scored 38 goals but underperformed their xG, ranking poorly in possession and final third entries. Las Palmas (19th, 32 points) have scored 40 goals but conceded 59, with a similar struggle in creating big chances.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Espanyol’s Javi Puado is a standout with 11 goals this season, while Las Palmas miss key forward Fábio Silva (10 goals). Absences on both sides could limit attacking output, especially for Las Palmas.

sections.subtitle: Match Context and Prediction Basis
sections.text: With both teams in relegation danger and struggling for form, this match is likely to be cautious and low-scoring. Espanyol’s home advantage and slightly better defensive stats give them a slight edge.
